title: schoenus centralis
intro: Schoenus centralis, also known as the northern bog sedge, is a type of perennial plant native to wetland areas in New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Schoenus centralis include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in the treatment of urinary problems. In herbal preparations, Schoenus centralis is often used in the form of a tincture or infusion, and can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
